description: Editable reference examples for Complaint Intake symptom classification. These examples are embedded and compared with incoming complaint text before any LLM fallback is used.

categories.description: The displayed or calculated clinical value is static, repeating, frozen at a prior value, or not changing when the patient condition changes.
examples: The oxygen saturation value stayed at the exact same number for the entire observation.
The displayed pulse and SpO2 values appear frozen on the previous measurement.
The monitor keeps repeating the old reading instead of changing with the patient.
The numeric vitals are locked at one value even after the probe is moved.
The reading remains fixed at 98 percent and never varies during monitoring.
The same measurement is shown over multiple checks with no visible change.
The pulse oximeter repeats an earlier value instead of producing a fresh measurement.
The measurement value is stuck and does not respond to signal changes.

categories.description: The measurement, display, or sampling loop is stale, delayed, or no longer refreshing from a live source.
examples: The screen still shows an old value after the sensor condition changes.
The measurement page is stale and does not refresh during monitoring.
Updated vitals are not pushed to the display after a new sample is taken.
The display lags behind the live sensor output for several minutes.
The data refresh appears stopped even though the device remains powered.
The reading update loop seems stalled after continuous use.
New pulse data is collected but the shown value is not refreshed.
The displayed measurement does not update after reconnecting the probe.

categories.description: The device provides a value, but the value appears wrong, clinically implausible, offset, drifting, or inconsistent with a trusted reference.
examples: The SpO2 value is much higher than the hospital reference monitor.
The pulse reading is far below the manually counted pulse rate.
The oxygen saturation number is clearly incorrect for the patient's condition.
The device reports an impossible measurement while the patient is stable.
The displayed reading drifts away from the calibrated reference device.
The pulse oximeter gives unreliable values during normal use.
The SpO2 output is lower than expected when checked against another monitor.
The numeric reading appears offset from the actual clinical measurement.

categories.description: The primary clinical reading, signal, value, or waveform is absent, blank, not detected, unavailable, or not displayed at all.
examples: No oxygen saturation number is visible on the device.
The SpO2 field is empty and never shows a measured value.
The pulse value does not appear anywhere on the screen.
The monitor shows dashes instead of a clinical reading.
The device cannot obtain a pulse signal from the connected probe.
The measurement area is blank even though monitoring has started.
The oximeter does not display a saturation value for the patient.
The clinical reading is unavailable and the screen shows no number.

categories.description: The screen or display is black, blank, dark, unlit, or visually unavailable.
examples: The screen goes completely black during use.
The display is dark even though the device seems powered.
The user interface is not visible because the backlight is off.
The monitor face is blank with no visible screen content.
The screen remains black after the device is turned on.
The display panel loses all visible output.
The device lights are on but the screen itself is blank.
The screen is fully dark and cannot be read.

categories.description: The display flickers, flashes, tears, shows artifacts, or has unstable visual output while still visible.
examples: The screen flickers whenever the device is monitoring.
The display flashes rapidly and makes the numbers hard to read.
The user interface shows visual artifacts across the measurement page.
The screen brightness pulses irregularly during operation.
The display image tears and the reading is difficult to see.
The panel intermittently blinks while values are shown.
The numeric display jitters on the screen.
The device screen has garbled visual output.

categories.description: The display or user interface freezes, stops responding, or remains stuck on a visual screen state.
examples: The user interface freezes after the measurement page opens.
The screen stops responding and stays on the same menu.
The display becomes frozen after a software update.
The device is powered but the visible screen state is locked.
The touchscreen page stops moving and no controls respond.
The display freezes while the device continues running.
The interface is stuck on the startup screen.
The screen locks up and cannot be navigated.

categories.description: An alarm, alert, or notification is delayed, late, absent for too long, or delivered after the expected clinical trigger.
examples: The low saturation alarm sounded several seconds late.
The notification was delayed after the patient crossed the alarm limit.
The alert did not arrive promptly during monitoring.
The alarm response lagged behind the displayed vital sign change.
The caregiver warning came after the event had already occurred.
The device waited too long before raising an alert.
Alarm notification latency was longer than expected.
The warning tone was late when oxygen saturation dropped.

categories.description: The device shuts down, restarts, loses power, fails to power on, or has battery or charging failure behavior.
examples: The device shuts down during patient monitoring.
The unit restarts by itself after a few minutes of use.
The pulse oximeter loses power even with battery charge remaining.
The device will not turn on after charging overnight.
The battery drains suddenly during normal operation.
Power is lost when the probe is connected.
The device cycles off and on without user input.
The charging indicator appears but the unit does not start.

categories.description: The complaint involves drop, impact, cracked housing, broken parts, connector damage, liquid ingress, or other physical damage.
examples: The device stopped working after it was dropped from the bed rail.
The housing cracked near the probe connector.
The unit was damaged after an accidental impact.
The display cover broke after the device fell onto the floor.
The probe port is loose after rough handling.
The casing is split and the device no longer reads correctly.
Liquid entered the unit and the controls became unreliable.
The device has visible damage after transport.
